tests: POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should assign reviewers whose expertise overlaps with paper topic areas and return assignments with assignmentId, paperId, and reviewerId
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should not assign a reviewer whose expertise does not overlap with the paper topic areas
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should not assign a reviewer who has a declared conflict with any of the paper authors
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should list papers with zero eligible reviewers in unassignedPaperIds
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should list papers that received fewer than reviewersPerPaper eligible reviewers in underAssignedPaperIds
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should default reviewersPerPaper to 3 when the body does not specify it
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should distribute assignments as evenly as possible across eligible reviewers
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should produce the same assignment count when auto-assign is re-run, replacing previous auto assignments
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should count existing manual assignments toward the reviewersPerPaper target
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should assign up to reviewersPerPaper eligible reviewers to each paper
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assign (auto-assignment) > should return 403 for non-chair roles
Re-running auto-assign preserves manual assignments > should keep manually-created assignments intact when auto-assign is re-run
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assignments (manual) > should create a manual assignment and return 201 with assignmentId, paperId, and reviewerId
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assignments (manual) > should return 403 for non-chair roles
DELETE /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assignments/:assignmentId > should delete an assignment and return 200 with a message
DELETE /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assignments/:assignmentId > should return 404 when the assignment does not exist
DELETE /api/conferences/:conferenceId/assignments/:assignmentId > should return 403 for non-chair roles
GET /api/reviewers/assignments > should return assigned papers with paperId, title, conferenceId, pdfUrl, and reviewed boolean
GET /api/reviewers/assignments > should set reviewed to true after a review is submitted for the assigned paper
GET /api/reviewers/assignments > should return assignments from multiple conferences for a single reviewer
GET /api/reviewers/assignments > should return 403 for non-reviewer roles
POST /api/auth/register > should register a new user and return 201 with a signed JWT token and a user object containing id, name, email, and role
POST /api/auth/register > should register users with each of the three roles: chair, reviewer, author
POST /api/auth/login > should authenticate with valid credentials and return 200 with a JWT token and user object
POST /api/auth/login > should return 401 for an unrecognized email address
POST /api/auth/login > should return 401 for an incorrect password
JWT authentication middleware > should return 401 when the Authorization header is absent
JWT authentication middleware > should return 401 when the token is invalid or malformed
JWT authentication middleware > should return 401 when the token is expired
POST /api/papers/:paperId/rebuttal > should submit a rebuttal and return 201 with paperId, rebuttalText, and submittedAt timestamp
POST /api/papers/:paperId/rebuttal > should return 409 when a final decision has already been recorded for the paper
POST /api/papers/:paperId/rebuttal > should return 403 with error message when the rebuttal period has closed (after notificationDate)
POST /api/papers/:paperId/rebuttal > should return 403 for a user who is not the paper submitting author
GET /api/papers/:paperId/rebuttal > should return the stored rebuttal text and submittedAt for the chair role
GET /api/papers/:paperId/rebuttal > should return 404 when no rebuttal has been submitted for the paper
GET /api/papers/:paperId/rebuttal > should return 403 for non-chair roles
GET /api/papers/:paperId/reviews-for-rebuttal > should return anonymized reviews with scores and recommendation, excluding confidentialNote, during the rebuttal phase
GET /api/papers/:paperId/reviews-for-rebuttal > should return 403 when a final decision has already been recorded
GET /api/papers/:paperId/reviews-for-rebuttal > should return 403 when the current time is after the conference notificationDate
GET /api/papers/:paperId/reviews-for-rebuttal > should return 403 for a user who is not the paper submitting author
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should submit a paper via multipart form-data and return 201 with paperId, title, conferenceId, and status "submitted"
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should accept a submission without the optional keywords field
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should return 403 for non-author roles attempting to submit a paper
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should return 403 with error message when the submission deadline has passed
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should generate a unique paperId for each submitted paper
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should return all papers for the conference with full metadata when requested by chair
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should return an empty array when no papers have been submitted to the conference
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should include the decision field for papers that have received a chair decision
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/papers > should return 403 for non-chair roles
GET /api/papers/:paperId > should return the paper record with metadata and pdfUrl, and include authors for the chair role
GET /api/papers/:paperId > should include authors field when requested by the submitting author
GET /api/papers/:paperId > should omit authors field when requested by a reviewer to preserve review integrity
POST /api/papers/:paperId/decision > should record an accept decision and return paperId, decision "accept", and status "accepted"
POST /api/papers/:paperId/decision > should record a reject decision and return status "rejected"
POST /api/papers/:paperId/decision > should persist the decision and update the paper status, verifiable via GET paper
POST /api/papers/:paperId/decision > should return 403 for non-chair roles
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/stats > should return aggregate stats with totalPapers, accepted, rejected, acceptanceRate, and scoreDistributions per criterion
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/stats > should return acceptanceRate as 0 when totalPapers is 0
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/stats > should return 403 for non-chair roles
GET /api/papers/:paperId/notification > should return decision "pending" and decisionFinal false when no decision has been recorded
GET /api/papers/:paperId/notification > should return reviews even before a final decision is recorded, allowing rebuttal-phase viewing
GET /api/papers/:paperId/notification > should return decisionFinal true and the final decision after the chair records it
GET /api/papers/:paperId/notification > should include reviewText, per-criterion scores, and recommendation in each review, and exclude confidentialNote
GET /api/papers/:paperId/notification > should include reviews alongside the final decision when both exist
GET /api/papers/:paperId/notification > should include a reviews array in the response even when no reviews have been submitted
GET /api/papers/:paperId/notification > should return 403 for a user who is not the paper submitting author
POST /api/papers/:paperId/reviews > should submit a review and return 201 with reviewId, paperId, reviewerId, and recommendation
POST /api/papers/:paperId/reviews > should return 403 for non-reviewer roles attempting to submit a review
POST /api/papers/:paperId/reviews > should return 403 when the reviewer has no assignment for that paper
GET /api/papers/:paperId/reviews > should return all reviews with complete fields including confidentialNote for the chair
GET /api/papers/:paperId/reviews > should persist all score fields and return matching values when retrieved by the chair
GET /api/papers/:paperId/reviews > should return 403 for non-chair roles
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/program > should create conference sessions and return 201 with conferenceId, sessions containing sessionId, details, and paper titles
POST /api/conferences/:conferenceId/program > should return 403 for non-chair roles
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/program > should return the full conference program with sessions and associated paper details
GET /api/conferences/:conferenceId/program > should be accessible by any authenticated user including reviewers
POST /api/papers/:paperId/camera-ready > should upload a camera-ready PDF and return 200 with paperId, status "camera_ready", and cameraReadyUrl
POST /api/papers/:paperId/camera-ready > should return 403 when the paper decision is not "accept"
POST /api/papers/:paperId/camera-ready > should return 403 when no decision has been recorded for the paper
POST /api/papers/:paperId/camera-ready > should return 403 with error message when the camera-ready deadline has passed
POST /api/papers/:paperId/camera-ready > should update the paper status to "camera_ready", verifiable via GET paper
POST /api/papers/:paperId/camera-ready > should return 403 for a user who is not the paper submitting author
POST /api/conferences > should create a conference and return 201 with id and all provided fields including topicAreas as a JSON array
POST /api/conferences > should return 403 for non-chair roles
GET /api/conferences/:id > should return the full conference record including all stored fields by ID
GET /api/conferences/:id > should return 404 for a non-existent conference ID
GET /api/conferences/:id > should be accessible by any authenticated user, not only chairs
PUT /api/reviewers/profile > should save the reviewer profile and return 200 with reviewerId, expertise array, and conflicts array
PUT /api/reviewers/profile > should return 403 for non-reviewer roles
GET /api/reviewers/profile > should return the authenticated reviewer stored expertise and conflicts arrays
GET /api/reviewers/profile > should return 403 for non-reviewer roles
